title: Resolve merge conflicts for Feature Root: Bead Graph Diagram Command
description:
Merge conflict detected during preflight merge of main into feature/b-0513c78c.
Conflicted files: src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py

Git error: Auto-merging src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py
Auto-merging tests/test_orchestrator.py
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

Diff context:
diff --cc src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py
index aea2ddf,5c24973..0000000
--- a/src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py
+++ b/src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py
@@@ -11,8 -10,7 +11,12 @@@ from pathlib import Pat
  
  from .config import load_config
  from .console import ConsoleReporter, SpinnerPool
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +from .graph import render_bead_graph
 +from .gitutils import WorktreeManager
++=======
+ from .gitutils import GitError, WorktreeManager
++>>>>>>> main
  from .models import Bead
  from .planner import PlanningService
  from .runner import ClaudeCodeAgentRunner, CodexAgentRunner

handoff_summary.completed: Added `GitError` to the gitutils import in cli.py, resolving the 3-way merge conflict. The feature branch now agrees with main on the gitutils import, so the next preflight merge should apply main's `command_merge` additions (--skip-rebase, --skip-tests, test gate) cleanly.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — conflict is resolved and committed.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The remaining diff between main and feature branch (command_merge additions, --skip-rebase/--skip-tests args) were not in the merge base, so git 3-way merge will take them cleanly from main.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry the merge: orchestrator merge B-0513c78c
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — the resolved import now matches main's gitutils import, eliminating the conflict hunk.

conflict_risks: None — the resolved import now matches main's gitutils import, eliminating the conflict hunk.

execution_history.summary: Resolved the merge conflict in src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py. The conflict was between the feature branch adding `from .graph import render_bead_graph` and main changing `from .gitutils import WorktreeManager` to `from .gitutils import GitError, WorktreeManager`. The resolution includes both: `from .gitutils import GitError, WorktreeManager` alongside the existing `from .graph import render_bead_graph` import. The `GitError` import is needed because main's `command_merge` uses it (preflight merge conflict detection), and it will be properly utilized when the feature branch merges with main's extended `command_merge` implementation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
